frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value updating driven by direct cached reward associations

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value updating via internal model of stimulus-outcome structure

frameworks.name: dual-process evaluation
frameworks.one_line: explicit vs implicit evaluations as two separate cognitive systems

frameworks.name: evaluative conditioning
frameworks.one_line: stimulus-stimulus pairing as mechanism for acquiring evaluations

core_question: Do explicit and implicit evaluations arise from distinct learning processes, specifically model-free versus model-based reinforcement learning?
key_assumptions: evaluation is a fundamental good-bad computation
model-free vs model-based RL distinction maps onto evaluation acquisition
revaluation paradigms can dissociate model-free from model-based updating
manipulations: number of stimulus exposures in revaluation phase
key_findings: explicit evaluations update via both model-free and model-based processes
implicit evaluations update only via model-free, not model-based, processes
dissociation robust across exposure counts and reinforcement schedules
interpretations: implicit evaluations are context-sensitive yet recalcitrant to inference-based updating
framework moves beyond dual-process and single-process accounts of evaluation
